keyPoints: Friction-design patterns framed as deliberate scaffolds to amplify human agency with robust evaluation and oversight.
Target question and real-world tasks clarified: constrained vs unconstrained prompts across coding, design, writing, and marketing with multi-model benchmarking.
Concise taxonomy of patterns presented (timeboxing, forced recall, constraint-based prompts/RTCF, CoT/ToT, GPS, multilingual prompts, activation-steering) with practical implications.
Gains framed as task- and model-dependent; require staged collaboration and cross-model validation.
Concrete design heuristics for practitioners (RTCF baseline, CoT/ToT for reasoning, TTCT-inspired metrics) and a structured evaluation rubric.
Failure modes and countermeasures highlighted (homogenization, over-trust, mode collapse; deliberate difficulty, diverse outputs, reflective prompts, light 'Dopamine Nutrition Label' ideas).
Roadmap for actionable outcomes provided: friction-pattern library, two-phase workflows, cross-domain validation, and explicit discussion of limitations.
